title: Clearly THE most in-depth British Open review
summary: In this episode of Make Me Good At Golf, Pete Helliar, Damien Fleming and Lehmo take a hilariously sharp swing at The British Open, breaking down the drama, duffs and dazzling moments from the iconic tournament. The trio celebrates Scottie Scheffler’s jaw-dropping fourth major win with a mix of awe and Aussie banter, questioning if he’s actually human - or just built in a golf lab. Whether you watched every hole or just the highlights, this episode is your comedic Open recap fix. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Introducing: Make Me Good At Golf
summary: Make Me Good At Golf is the hilarious new podcast where Aussie comedians Pete Helliar and Lehmo team up with cricket legend Damien Fleming to tackle their shared obsession: golf. Each episode follows the trio’s chaotic and comedic journey to become better golfers - if not actually good, at least slightly less terrible. With a mix of banter, golf fails, tips from the pros, and deep dives into their love-hate relationship with the game, they explore what makes golf so addictive (and maddening). In this debut episode, they confess their golfing sins, lay out their goals for self-improvement, and set the tone for a season of laughs, shanks, and surprising insights. To wrap things up, they preview the upcoming British Open, offering predictions, wishful thinking, and a fair bit of nonsense. Whether you’re a seasoned golfer or just golf-curious, this show is your new clubhouse hangout. Follow us on Youtube Instagram TikTok Facebook  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.
